title: How mizerShelf extends mizer
text: mizerShelf extension package mizer adds two extra ecosystem components standard multispecies size-spectrum model: detritus — particulate organic matter settles seafloor provides food small benthic organisms juvenile fish, carrion — fresh animal tissue natural deaths, fishing gear mortality, discards consumed scavengers decomposing detritus. components dynamical: biomasses change time response species abundances fishing effort. package uses four five extension mechanisms described vignette("extensions", package = "mizer"): four mechanisms wired together inside newDetritusCarrionParams(), single entry point building shelf model.

title: How mizerShelf extends mizer
text: mizerShelf defines two S4 marker subclasses: classes carry extra slots. purpose trigger S3 dispatch shelf-specific behaviour used automatically whenever user calls mizer generic mizerShelf object. classes marker classes sense described vignette("extensions", package = "mizer"). newDetritusCarrionParams() records extension params@extensions coerces result mizerShelf marker class: Recording extension params@extensions allows mizer automatically produce mizerShelfSim whenever project() called: mizer’s MizerSim() constructor calls coerceToExtensionClass(), reads params@extensions, sees mizerShelf, coerces new sim object mizerShelfSim.
code: setClass("mizerShelf",    contains = "MizerParams") setClass("mizerShelfSim", contains = "MizerSim") params@extensions <- c(mizerShelf = as.character(packageVersion("mizerShelf"))) new("mizerShelf", params)

title: How mizerShelf extends mizer
text: standard getBiomass() returns species biomasses. shelf version appends detritus carrion biomasses: plotBiomass() calls getBiomass() internally, single override makes biomass plots show detritus carrion alongside fish species without changes.
code: getBiomass.mizerShelf <- function(object, ...) {     params <- object     b <- NextMethod()                               # standard species biomasses     d_biomass <- sum(params@initial_n_pp *                      params@dw_full * params@w_full) # integrate resource spectrum     b <- c(b, Detritus = d_biomass)     scalar_other <- Filter(         function(x) is.numeric(x) && length(x) == 1,         params@initial_n_other)     if (length(scalar_other) > 0) b <- c(b, unlist(scalar_other))     b } getBiomass(NWMed_params)[1:3] #>     Small DF worms Small DF crustacea           DF worms  #>          0.6514945          0.1603410          0.7829609 sim <- project(NWMed_params, t_max = 3, t_save = 0.5, progress_bar = FALSE) plotBiomass(sim, species = c("Nut clam", "Detritus")) #> Warning: Removed 7 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`).

title: How mizerShelf extends mizer
text: Mizer’s standard resource spectrum follows semi-chemostat towards carrying capacity. mizerShelf replaces detritus_dynamics(), registered via resource_dynamics argument newMultispeciesParams(): lightest-weight way change resource reuses mizer’s resource infrastructure — size grid, resource abundance array n_pp, resource_mort rate, — replacing update step.

title: How mizerShelf extends mizer
text: detritus spectrum always held fixed power-law shape; overall biomass BB dynamical. biomass satisfies dBdt=production−consumption⋅B+external\frac{dB}{dt} = \text{production} - \text{consumption} \cdot B + \text{external} : production comes unassimilated food (faeces), decomposing carrion, external input pelagic zone (getDetritusProduction()), consumption total rate species feed detritus (detritus_consumption()), proportional current biomass, external tunable constant represents sedimentation pelagic waters, stored other_params(params)$detritus$external. detritus_dynamics() solves ODE analytically within time step avoid instabilities simple Euler step: B(t+Δt)=B(t)e−cΔt+pc(1−e−cΔt)B(t + \Delta t) = B(t)\,e^{-c\,\Delta t} + \frac{p}{c}\left(1 - e^{-c\,\Delta t}\right) cc mass-specific consumption rate pp production rate. shape spectrum scaled new biomass: function receives n_pp (current resource spectrum), initial spectrum, update applied whatever state simulation currently .
code: detritus_dynamics <- function(params, n, n_pp, n_other, rates, dt, ...) {     current_biomass <- detritus_biomass(params, n_pp = n_pp)     consumption     <- detritus_consumption(params, n_pp, rates) / current_biomass     production      <- sum(getDetritusProduction(params, n, n_other, rates))     if (consumption) {         et           <- exp(-consumption * dt)         next_biomass <- current_biomass * et + production / consumption * (1 - et)     } else {         next_biomass <- current_biomass + production * dt     }     n_pp * next_biomass / current_biomass }

title: How mizerShelf extends mizer
text: carrion biomass BB satisfies ODE structure detritus: dBdt=production−consumption⋅B\frac{dB}{dt} = \text{production} - \text{consumption} \cdot B fraction ext_prop natural (non-predation) mortality, excess gear mortality (gearMort()), .e. animals killed gear landed, discards — landed animals thrown back. consumption (carrion_consumption_ms()) includes active consumption fish scavenging carrion plus bacterial decomposition. detritus, ODE solved analytically within time step:
code: carrion_dynamics <- function(params, n, n_other, rates, dt, ...) {     consumption <- carrion_consumption_ms(params, n, rates)     production  <- sum(getCarrionProduction(params, n, rates))     if (consumption) {         et <- exp(-consumption * dt)         return(n_other$carrion * et + production / consumption * (1 - et))     }     return(n_other$carrion + production * dt) }

title: How mizerShelf extends mizer
text: Standard mizer fishing mortality accounts fish extracted water. Continental shelf fisheries also kill animals remain seabed contact bottom-trawl gear. mizerShelf captures distinction custom mortality function registered via setRateFunction(): seMort() calls standard mizerMort() adds excess gear mortality top: gearMort() returns max(gear_mort - f_mort, 0), gear_mort species parameter giving total rate animals die due gear contact. fishing mortality cap, remainder enters total mortality also enters carrion production (via getCarrionProduction()). fishing mortality exceeds gear_mort, excess gear mortality zero. custom rate function receives returns exactly array shape mizerMort() (species × size), drop-replacement adds one ecological process without changing anything else.
code: params <- setRateFunction(params, "Mort", "seMort") seMort <- function(params, n, n_pp, n_other, t, f_mort, pred_mort, ...) {     mizerMort(params, n, n_pp, n_other, t, f_mort, pred_mort, ...) +         gearMort(params, f_mort = f_mort) }

what: How the pieces fit together in newDetritusCarrionParams()
title: How mizerShelf extends mizer
text: four extension mechanisms wired inside single constructor: order matters. resource dynamics set inside newMultispeciesParams() mizer computes initial rates, detritus shape consistent start. carrion component registered species parameters set, rho can computed initial encounter rates. marker class applied last, standard mizer slots populated.
code: newDetritusCarrionParams <- function(species_params,                                      w_min_detritus = NA,                                      w_max_detritus = 1,                                      n = 0.7, ...) {     # 1. Detritus: override resource dynamics     params <- newMultispeciesParams(         species_params   = species_params,         min_w_pp         = w_min_detritus,         w_pp_cutoff      = w_max_detritus,         n = n, p = n,         resource_dynamics = "detritus_dynamics",         ...)      # Initialise rho so each species can reach feeding level f0 at max size     f0  <- set_species_param_default(params@species_params, "f0", 0.6)$f0     ic  <- set_species_param_default(                params@species_params, "interaction_carrion", 1)$interaction_carrion     E   <- getEncounter(params)[, length(params@w)] /                (params@w[length(params@w)] ^ n)     rho <- pmax(0, f0 * params@species_params$h / (1 - f0) - E) * ic     params@species_params$rho_carrion <- rho     rho <- outer(params@species_params$rho_carrion, params@w ^ n)      # 2. Mortality: custom rate function adds excess gear mortality     params <- setRateFunction(params, "Mort", "seMort")      # 3. Carrion: scalar dynamical component     params <- setComponent(         params, "carrion", initial_value = 1,         dynamics_fun     = "carrion_dynamics",         encounter_fun    = "encounter_contribution",         component_params = list(rho = rho))      # 4. Marker class: enables S3 dispatch for shelf-specific methods     params <- setColours(params,                          c(Detritus = "forestgreen", carrion = "peru"))     new("mizerShelf", params) }

title: How mizerShelf extends mizer
text: mizerShelf illustrates four core mizer extension mechanisms working together: Resource dynamics override — detritus reuses mizer’s resource spectrum infrastructure custom ODE tracks total biomass rescales spectrum shape. setRateFunction() — custom seMort() adds excess gear mortality standard mortality calculation single-line wrapper around mizerMort(). setComponent() — carrion scalar dynamical variable whose biomass evolves time step whose encounter-rate contribution automatically included getEncounter() getDiet(). S4 marker classes + S3 dispatch — mizerShelf mizerShelfSim ensure shelf-specific methods (getBiomass, steady, scaleModel, addSpecies, removeSpecies) dispatched automatically, every override delegates NextMethod() full mizer pipeline remains intact. Together, mechanisms allow mizerShelf add two ecologically important processes — benthic detritus carrion scavenging — mizer model without modifying mizer source code.

title: Carrion dynamics — carrion_dynamics
text: time evolution carrion biomass \(B\) described $$dB/dt = \tt{production} - \tt{consumption} * B$$ consumption mass-specific rate consumption due consumption calculated carrion_consumption_ms(), production rate rest system produces carrion biomass, calculated getCarrionProduction(). dynamical equation solved analytically $$B(t+dt) = B(t)\exp(-\tt{consumption} \cdot dt)   +\frac{\tt{production}}{\tt{consumption}}   (1-\exp(-\tt{consumption} \cdot dt)).$$ avoids stability problems arise used Euler method solve equation numerically.
